seo_description: Current time in Caracas, Venezuela — live Venezuela Time (VET, UTC-4) clock, no DST, and time differences with New York, Bogota, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Caracas right now?
faq.answer: Caracas, Venezuela uses Venezuela Time (VET, UTC-4) year-round. Venezuela does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Caracas moved to UTC-4:30 in 2007 and then back to UTC-4 in 2016. The IANA timezone identifier is America/Caracas.

faq.question: What time zone is Caracas in?
faq.answer: Caracas is in the Venezuela Time zone (VET, UTC-4). Venezuela maintains UTC-4 year-round with no DST. Caracas shares its timezone with La Paz (Bolivia) and parts of Canada (Atlantic Standard Time in winter). The IANA identifier is America/Caracas.

faq.question: Does Caracas observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Venezuela does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Caracas remains on VET (UTC-4) year-round. Venezuela briefly used UTC-4:30 from 2007 to 2016 before reverting to UTC-4.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Caracas and New York?
faq.answer: Caracas (VET, UTC-4) is 1 hour ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US Daylight Saving Time (EDT, UTC-4), Caracas and New York are on the same time. Since Venezuela has no DST, the gap shifts between 0 and 1 hour depending on whether the US is observing DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Caracas and Bogota?
faq.answer: Caracas (VET, UTC-4) is 1 hour ahead of Bogota (COT, UTC-5). Colombia does not observe DST, and neither does Venezuela, so this 1-hour gap is constant year-round.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Caracas and London?
faq.answer: Caracas (VET, UTC-4) is 4 hours behind London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1), Caracas is 5 hours behind London. Since Venezuela has no DST, the gap changes only when London transitions its clocks.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Caracas?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Caracas are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM VET, Monday through Friday. Caracas is Venezuela's capital and its largest city. The Caracas Stock Exchange (BVC) operates Monday–Friday. Venezuela's economy has historically been dominated by oil (PDVSA), though the country has faced severe economic challenges. Caracas is also a center for regional government, media, and banking for Venezuela.
